name: Ulsan (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: the sea, the Taehwa River, and Ulsan's identity as Korea's industrial port

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem in blue and red, depicting an abstract whale-and-wave motif together with the letters spelling Ulsan in flowing form.
history: Ulsan, Korea's seventh-largest city and the country's principal industrial port, became a Metropolitan City (Gwangyeoksi) on 15 July 1997. The current city flag and emblem were adopted at the same time. The whale-and-wave motif recalls the prehistoric Bangudae petroglyphs (a UNESCO-listed site near the city) which include some of the world's earliest depictions of whales, and Ulsan's modern role as a centre of shipbuilding and heavy industry.

features.role: Ulsan city emblem — whale, wave and city name
features.symbolism: abstract whale-and-wave motif together with stylised letters spelling Ulsan; the whale recalls the prehistoric Bangudae petroglyphs and the city's modern shipbuilding industry, while the wave evokes the Taehwa River and the East Sea
